title: dendranthema naktongense
intro: Dendranthema naktongense, also known as Korean wormwood, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Korean Peninsula and China. The medicinal properties of Dendranthema naktongense include its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its essential oil components such as camphor, borneol, and borneol acetate. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and treat respiratory infections, making it a valuable traditional remedy in East Asian medicine. Dendranthema naktongense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
